computation_name: Blocking Candidate Generation for Pairwise Similarity
definition: Partition train and evaluation records into blocks using keys, sorted neighborhoods, canopies, or metadata filters, then compute exact similarity only within candidate blocks. In SPECTRA, blocking is useful when the scientific similarity is conditional on matching site, assay, lineage, cohort, or another coarse attribute.

scale_profile.best_for: Record-like data where cheap keys remove impossible or irrelevant comparisons.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions of records when block sizes are controlled.
scale_profile.avoid_when: The blocking key is itself the scientific generalization axis and would hide cross-block novelty.

required_inputs: blocking columns or cheap blocking rule

tunable_parameters.name: max_block_size
tunable_parameters.purpose: Prevents common keys from recreating all-pairs comparisons.

tunable_parameters.name: within_block_similarity
tunable_parameters.purpose: Exact scorer applied to candidates in the same block.

quality_gates: Report candidate reduction and estimated pair recall.
Check large blocks and split them or handle separately.
Confirm the blocking rule does not remove the novelty axis the audit is meant to measure.
failure_modes: Bad keys can miss true similar pairs.
Very common keys can create oversized blocks.
Blocking on a leakage variable can make the audit too easy or scientifically invalid.
example_usage: python blocking_candidate_similarity.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --block-cols site,assay --token-col features

citations.title: A survey of blocking and filtering techniques for entity resolution
citations.evidence: Frames pairwise comparison as the dominant cost in entity resolution and surveys blocking/filtering to reduce candidates.

citations.title: Parallel Sorted Neighborhood Blocking with MapReduce
citations.evidence: Uses sorted-neighborhood blocking to reduce comparisons in parallel entity-resolution workflows.
